vulnerabilities.description: SQL query constructed using string concatenation with user input. This allows an attacker to inject arbitrary SQL commands.
User input passed directly to os.system() shell command. An attacker could execute arbitrary system commands.
User input rendered directly in HTML output without escaping. Allows cross-site scripting attacks.
API key hardcoded as string literal in source code. If committed to version control, this exposes the key to all repository viewers.
MD5 used for password hashing. MD5 is cryptographically broken and can be brute-forced in seconds.
eval() called on user-controlled input. An attacker can execute arbitrary Python code.
User input used directly in file path without validation. An attacker can access files outside the intended directory using ../ sequences.
random module used for security token generation. The random module is not cryptographically secure.
DEBUG = True hardcoded in application configuration. Exposes stack traces and debug info to attackers in production.
pickle.loads() called on untrusted data. An attacker can craft a pickle payload that executes arbitrary code.
User input written directly to log output without sanitization. Attacker can inject fake log entries or corrupt log files.
JWT signature verification disabled. An attacker can forge tokens with arbitrary claims.
User-supplied URL used directly in redirect without validation. Attacker can redirect users to malicious sites.
XML parser does not disable external entity processing. An attacker can read local files or perform SSRF via crafted XML.
State-changing endpoint lacks CSRF token validation. An attacker can trick authenticated users into performing unintended actions.
File upload without extension or MIME type validation. Attacker can upload executable files.
Access-Control-Allow-Origin set to wildcard (*) with credentials. Any website can make authenticated requests.
HTTP responses lack security headers (CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options). Leaves the application vulnerable to various client-side attacks.
Regex pattern with nested quantifiers susceptible to catastrophic backtracking. Attacker can cause denial of service with crafted input.
User input inserted directly into LDAP search filter. Attacker can modify the filter to bypass authentication or extract data.
User input concatenated into XPath expression. Attacker can modify the query to access unauthorized data.
